title: I used AI to help write my research paper — ethically? Here's my take
post:
I used AI to help write my last research paper. Grammar checking, structure suggestions, clarity improvements. And I disclosed it in my methods section. The reaction from peers was... mixed.

Here's my position: using AI for editing and refinement is no different than using a professional editor. The ideas were mine. The analysis was mine. The AI handled the mechanical parts. Where I draw the line: using AI to generate findings or interpret results. That's not editing — that's fabrication.

The research community hasn't caught up with these tools yet. But we will — and we need to have honest conversations about where the lines are.

What's your stance on AI use in academic writing? Thoughts?
